_note: Searchable position and role glossary for a standalone soccer primer.

positions.label: Goalkeeper
positions.summary: The only player allowed to use hands inside the penalty area and the last line of defense.
positions.whatToWatch: Notice whether the keeper stays deep as a shot-stopper or steps higher like an extra passer.

positions.label: Center-back
positions.summary: The central defenders who protect the box, attack aerial balls, and keep the line organized.
positions.whatToWatch: A calm center-back often makes the whole team look calmer because everyone trusts the space behind them.

positions.label: Fullback
positions.summary: Wide defenders who protect the flank but often join attacks with overlaps and crosses.
positions.whatToWatch: If a fullback keeps flying forward, someone else usually has to cover the risk behind them.

positions.label: Defensive midfielder
positions.summary: The shield in front of the defense who breaks up attacks and keeps the team balanced.
positions.whatToWatch: Great defensive midfielders can dominate a game without taking many flashy touches.

positions.label: Central midfielder
positions.summary: The all-purpose connector who links defense to attack and helps control tempo.
positions.whatToWatch: The best central midfielders seem to appear everywhere just before the game tilts.

positions.label: Attacking midfielder
positions.summary: The creative player operating between midfield and the forwards, looking for killer passes and shots.
positions.whatToWatch: If a team suddenly starts finding dangerous passes through the middle, this player is often the reason.

positions.label: Winger
positions.summary: The wide attacker who stretches the field, isolates defenders, and creates danger from the flank.
positions.whatToWatch: When a crowd starts buzzing before a pass even arrives, it is often because the winger has space to attack.

positions.label: Striker
positions.summary: The central forward who leads the line, attacks the box, and is usually judged by finishing.
positions.whatToWatch: Even when the striker barely touches the ball, their movement can open space for everyone else.

positions.label: Wing-back
positions.summary: A wide player in back-three systems who has to cover almost the entire flank.
positions.whatToWatch: If the wing-back gets pinned deep, the whole formation can look more defensive than planned.
